reason: Strong on public contract hardening, but too narrow as-is because it underemphasizes architecture-boundary proof and adversarial lifecycle validation.

reason: Useful emphasis on contract triage, but not selected as-is because delaying runtime cleanup too much would leave the most failure-prone OBS lifecycle behavior implicit.
approach: Contract Triage Before Hardening: freeze the smallest externally visible surface first, then let runtime hardening follow only where it protects that surface.
selected_approach: Hybrid: Contract-Anchored Runtime Proof. Use public contract ambiguity as the first planning filter, then prioritize runtime hardening proofs where those contracts depend on observable failure behavior.
selected_reasoning: This should guide planning now because the project is past feature construction and into trust-building. The strongest next plan should not chase breadth, but it also should not treat fixtures, policy, and runtime cleanup as separate tracks. Public CLI/IPC contracts, daemon-first boundaries, JSON diagnostics, require-level behavior, canonical errors, and malformed OBS cleanup all define what downstream users and tests can rely on. Planning should first lock ambiguous external behavior enough...
